steps.content: Apply the triangle inequality theorem for all three pairs of sides. Let the known sides be 5 and 9, and the unknown side be $x$. The three conditions are: (1) $5 + 9 \gt x$, (2) $5 + x \gt 9$, and (3) $9 + x \gt 5$.
Simplify each inequality. From (1): $x \lt 14$. From (2): $x \gt 4$. From (3): $x \gt -4$, which is always true for any positive length.
Combine the binding constraints: $x \gt 4$ and $x \lt 14$, giving $4 \lt x \lt 14$.

• "$x \lt 4$": Allows only sides shorter than 4, but then $5 + x \lt 9$ violates the inequality. Eliminated.
  • "$x \gt 14$": Allows only sides longer than 14, but then $5 + 9 = 14$ is not greater than $x$. Eliminated.
  • "$4 \lt x \lt 14$": Satisfies all three triangle inequality conditions simultaneously. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
  • "$x \lt 4$ or $x \gt 14$": This is the union of the two eliminated ranges; neither region forms a valid triangle. Eliminated.
